metadata.phase: L11 armed-check — Helius getWalletTransfers (limit=20) on 22 candidates: 19 operator-flagged Sniper Network userEntity members + 22YY15 + 6zZ + DmA9
metadata.question: Which of these wallets are funded inside the L11 funding window (2026-04-22 onwards) and by whom?
metadata.tooling: Helius MCP getWalletTransfers via parallel calls

headline_finding.summary: SUSYE deployer (2Zizao3xpJGsoUFdvTJjYL8LijtFxR9v36cNBeDJJVSz) mass-armed 9 sniper wallets in a 12-minute window on 2026-04-22 12:59:11–13:11:17 UTC. All 9 transfers bracketed by 2Zi* vanity-cluster dust pings (signal-channel pattern). Same fingerprint as DmA9's known L11 pre-arm — at scale (9 wallets not 1).
headline_finding.implication: Decisive H1 evidence for the SUSYE/DmA9-side of the operation. A single funder pushing 9 wallets in 12 minutes with dust-ping signaling is operator-controlled infrastructure.
headline_finding.figg_side_coordination: Figg-side reload happened in the same broader L11 window: Figg → 22YY15 (1 SOL warm-up 2026-04-24 16:47:34, then 11 SOL main load 2026-04-25 12:57:49); Figg → 6zZ (0.1 SOL trickle at 2026-04-25 13:00:54, then 3.5 SOL main reload at 13:01:36). Figg's 6zZ reload happened 4 minutes after the 22YY15 main load — multi-rail loading.

behavioral_fingerprint_susye_l11_arming.common_signal: 2Zi* vanity-cluster dust pings (2Zi1apkSiDqArmrAwJdHwkVUn2BiqyiVV1ffG1tT3wSz, 2Ziz3B9rt8dbS1QanWQLqmTa5Erw4r7q79MR3UCgJVSz) within ~30s of each main funding event
behavioral_fingerprint_susye_l11_arming.interpretation: Operator-controlled mass-arming via single-source funder + signal-channel ping. Equivalent to DmA9's known L11 pre-arm pattern but at scale (9 wallets not 1).

events.signal: pcLM signal pinger ping at 12:57:51 (2s after main load)

events.comment: reload arrived 4 minutes after 22YY15 main load

h1_evidence_strengthened: SUSYE deployer is mass-arming 9+ wallets via a single funder in a tight 12-minute window with dust-ping signaling — operator-controlled infrastructure, not 9 independent paid insiders.
Two of the 9 SUSYE-armed wallets (HGBDX, HNtUfwr) show CoinSpot pre-fund + 9a22Fh profit drain + SUSYE re-arm patterns — the full deployer cycle.
Figg-side reload on 6zZ happened 4 minutes after Figg's 22YY15 main load, suggesting coordinated multi-rail loading rather than independent operator decisions.
h1_h2_implication.h2_residual_uncertainty: Figg-side and SUSYE-side could still be two separate operators paid by the deployer (insider-paid model). To distinguish H1-direct from H1-via-payment, terminus traces (Phase B/C) on the 9a22Fh accumulator and Figg-cluster cashout addresses are still needed. But the SUSYE side is unambiguously a single-operator multi-wallet rig regardless of the Figg link.
